subject: Anthropology
title: Domestication of Animals
description: A fast breakdown of Domestication of Animals for builders.

hook.text: Wolves followed human camps for scraps. Humans kept the friendliest ones. Thousands of years later: dogs.

buildup.text: Animal domestication began around 15,000 years ago with dogs, followed by sheep, goats, cattle, and pigs.

discovery.text: Domesticated animals provided meat, milk, wool, labor, and transport — transforming human economies and diets.

twist.text: Domestication changed animals too. Cows shrank, dogs diversified, and chickens lost their ability to fly far.

climax.text: Domestication was a partnership that reshaped both species. Humans changed animals and animals changed us.

punchline.text: We tamed animals; they transformed our world.

quiz.question: What was the first domesticated animal?
